text.content:
This document is intended to be player accessible. It contains the gangwide/corpwide abilities of every mook type, along with their areas of operation and general strategies. These are all things anyone who's walked the streets of Night City would know, particularly a savvy edgerunner.

Be sure to review the Homebrew Essentials document before running the mooks themselves; it contains item adjustments, and some minor rules updates which are made use of throughout the mook pack.

Have fun!

pages.name: So, you want to be an edgerunner, eh?

text.content:
The man in the ad has a malicious grin; Russian, with a greedy glint in his eye. You know him as Treasure Marcus, a fixer who operates out of Old Japantown and specializes in reclamation.

"So, you want to be an edgerunner, eh? Get up off the streets and show the world what you’re packing? I suggest you keep that package in your pants, gonked-up gutter trash. It’s a dangerous world out there, and there’s a reason there’s always a demand for new blood down at the Afterlife. They don’t call it that for nothing, eh? So give your mommy a kiss goodbye before you decide to take that scav-bust job, because odds are good that you won’t be coming back.

"But what do I know – I’m just the man who employs you. Treasure Marcus speaking – maybe you heard of me? I run reclaimer salvage teams all over the city, whether it’s dredging up shipwrecks off the coast, digging out campuses from the Old City Center or dusting off relics out in the Badlands. If you need something that doesn’t get made anymore, there’s only one way to get it. Me.

"Whether you’re here for scrap or glory, though, welcome to Night City, choom! Keep an eye on your pockets and two more on the streets, and maybe you’ll last the month. I’m sending you some basics on the big players, here, free of charge. What can I say, I’m a generous guy. And if gonked-out Smashbrains like you just so happen to create endless opportunity for profit after cleaning the bloodstains off of whatever you fight over… well that works out for us both, doesn’t it?

"So get out there and make some noise, my friend.  Make this city remember your name. Aaand if it ends up going poorly, well, don’t say I didn’t warn you, eh?"

There's some scum you find practically everywhere in Night City. Part of every gang, working for every corp. Rather than go over all of them individually, I thought I'd save us all some time and lump them all together right here. You're welcome.

All Generic Mooks

Generic street thugs and gangsters can be included as part of any gang in the pack; they gain that gang's gang-wide ability in addition to their own; their reputation also becomes the same as the reputation for the gang they're a part of.

Generic corporate security can be included as part of any corp in the pack, to the same effects.

Street Thugs

Not everyone has what it takes to make it in a boostergang, you know? And most of those people aren't good enough to work solo or hold down a job, either. Well? Where did you all the D students ended up after they dropped outta high school?

Territory

Everywhere, but especially plentiful in outlying districts, and scarcer on island districts.

What To Expect

Thugs are numerous, but flee quickly and probably won't stay to fight if the odds are against them, nor will they pick on anyone who's bigger than them. Typically they'll be a minor obstacle that can occasionally prove difficult to deal with, like any other kind of vermin.

Gang-Wide Ability - Well that was embarassing

Well that was embarrassing: If a player character is brought to 0 HP or killed due to the actions of a street thug, they lose 1 rep and the ganger responsible gains 1 rep.

Gangsters

Look, how do I say this… when the Tyger Claws decide to send their people over to Little Russia, they're not sending their best. They're bringing the crappier guns, they're shaking down businesses for drug money, and some, I assume, are the actual gang members.

Territory

Everywhere that gang activity is present; in Night City, that's just about everywhere, period.

What To Expect

Gangsters make up the bulk of any gang's forces - they're the disposable, numerous, rank-and-file meat that's fed to the grinder, keeping the body-count lotto unpredictable. They're often present as flunkies for actual gangsters, and their gang ability means that any actions that are taken against even the most unprepared targets needs to happen fast, or teams will find themselves outnumbered and outgunned very quickly.

Gang-Wide Ability - (Dis)Organized Crime

For every gangster in a scene, there’s a booster waiting somewhere nearby, just hoping for something to smash up. They enter the scene 1d3 rounds after hearing gunfire or being summoned.

Corporate Security

You smell that? It smells like rats. Thousands of them, breeding like wildfire, scurrying between the cubicles and the printer at the end of the office, biting and clawing at each other to stay in the light. Be careful when you're around them - you might lose a finger.

Territory

Common in corporate controlled (green) zones, usually appearing in teams of 4-6 for specialized projects. They keep low to the ground anywhere else - local gangs love the smell of fresh rat, it makes their own wallets bigger.

What To Expect

Anti-dazzle, level dampers and flashbangs are standard among RatSec agents, giving them formidable surprise rounds and the ability to keep opponents on the back foot. They're typically small teams that know the area well, but won't get caught up in an open fight - if they're attacked, they'll usually dig in and wait for the NCPD to arrive and do their dirty work for them. Drones make for good scouting targets, letting corpos pluck at exposed targets or deny objectives from edgerunners as those goals are discovered.

Corp-Wide Ability - Corp-Mandated Upgrades

All RatSec agents are equipped with Anti-Dazzle in both eyes and Level Damper in both ears, as well as internal agents. For every three agents in a scene, add a RatSec Mini Air Drone or Ground Drone to accompany them. They will always attempt to contact the NCPD when engaged, even if engaged in criminal action themselves. When arriving on the scene, the NCPD will always give priority to the accounts of RatSec, will not be hostile to them, and will take orders from Execs.

Want to make a name in Night City? You'll probably be running into these guys - they're in every Bodega, in the lobby of every cube hotel, watching every street corner. Fortunately, most of them don't bite unless you bite first… unless some asshole's feeling cocky, anyway.

I've known a lot of good people that were part of gangs. I've known a lot of terrible mass-murderers that were part of gangs. Take the good with the bad, average it out, and usually you'll end up with a paying customer at the end of the day, just how I prefer it.

6th Street

Ah, see, this is what I'm talking about. 6th Street keep their turf safe, keep the schools from getting shot up, and keep the real freaks from turning Night City into Nightmare on Jig-Jig Street. Aaaand they're also major drugs and arms smugglers, and fight a bloody war with anyone threatening their interests.

Territory

Strong presence in South Night City, Rancho Coronado, and South Heywood. Modest presence in the old combat zone, and minor presence in New Westbrook.

What To Expect

6th Streeters rely on guerilla tactics and aftermarket military kit, with most either being vets of CW4 or else trained by them directly. Once they're on a scene, they're quick to dig in like a tick, suppressing targets until they can go for the kill, only to pack up and leave nothing but razorwire and sandbags by the time the NCPD arrives.

They're well versed in their chosen strategy; expect a war of attrition amid a hail of suppressive fire, with heaps of collateral damage to boot.

Gang-Wide Ability - Guerilla Tactics

6th Street gang members can enter and leave partial cover without a penalty, and do not suffer penalties while firing out of it.

Maelstrom

I went home for the holidays a few years ago and these guys came up at the dinnertable. Known worldwide as the psychos that they are. Nowadays they mostly deal with weapons smuggling out of the NorCal military base - there's always someone willing to take a little cut in exchange for opening up a warehouse. And they're one of the only gangs that can actually deal with some of NC's more… environmentally hazardous areas.

Territory

Strong presence in Watson and the destroyed city center; moderate presence in Upper Marina, and minor presence in New Westbrook.

What To Expect

Maelstrom are a small, elite gang made up entirely of chromed out psychos, but which have a large following of posers (read: generic gangsters) who are just begging to be put under the knife and join the ranks of the city's biggest badasses. They don't negotiate, and usually shoot first - watch your head. In combat they usually operate relatively independently of each other, relying on individual power over group cohesion.

Gang-Wide Ability - Aftermarket Sandevistan

At the start of each round of initiative, choose a Maelstrom gang member currently in combat. That gang member goes first, this round. Additionally, when rolling endurance checks for Maelstrom, use the physical skill instead of lowdown.

Scavs

Human garbage, these, and every one of them is a psycho minus the cyber. You'd have to be to chop up your neighbors. And the bastards are everywhere; I swear half of my jobs are rescues or clearouts from this trash. If you ever see one, don't take your eyes off them - they'll be on you before you know it.

Territory

Strong presence in New Westbrook and the Old Combat Zone; moderate presence in Heywood, Rancho Coronado, South Night City and Watson. Minor presence just about everywhere else.

What To Expect

Scavs come to combat prepared to scrap, and take chunks out of their opponents while they fight - it only takes one lucky shot for the gang to turn a fight in their favor. In response to their brutish savagery, utilize precision, and above all else keep them at a distance.

Scavs tend to ambush targets with overwhelming numbers, subduing them and transporting them to their hideaways for their techs to take apart. These hideouts are mazes with an abundance of dark corners - don't become a victim in the rescue attempt.

Gang-Wide Ability - Untamed Savagery

On a successful crit, scavs always dismember a hand, arm, or leg, in place of any other result.

Tyger Claws

Ah, these I have the most experience with - I rent my apartment from a local leader. Not going to act like they're good people, but they're fiercely loyal to themselves and each other, and don't let just anyone join. I'd say their code is honorable, except for what happens when they decide you've breached it. Maybe you'll lose an arm, if you're lucky. If you're not, some poor gonk in the municipal will be cleaning your skinned carcass off the streets the next day.

Territory

Strong presence in Old Japan Town and Little China; moderate presence in the Glen, the Old Combat Zone, and Upper Marina. Minor presence in South Night City.

What To Expect

In place of numbers or conventional arms, Tygers have trained to use their bikes virtually anywhere, riding through narrow back alleys and even jumping up buildings onto rooftops to deliver a deadly strike that's over as soon as it starts. Be careful not to catch a front tire to the sternum; many an enemy of the gang has been left as a bloody smear on the pavement.

When holding territory, expect an abundance of low-level generic gangoons to hold the line - backed up by rip-roaring, kabuki-wearing professional criminals performing hit and runs on motorcycles.

Gang-Wide Ability - Speed Demons

While driving or riding in a vehicle, Tygers gain a +2 bonus to their REF and DEX. When a non-generic member of the Tyger Claws is in a scene, they're accompanied by a Tyger Oni; once the second seat on the Oni has been filled, the next member of the gang to appear is accompanied by another Oni, and so on.

While driving or riding an Oni, Tygers auto-pass any driving checks with a DV of 17 or less, and can dodge bullets using their technical skill. They can transfer from one moving vehicle to another adjacent one without making a check, unless such a check is opposed, in which case they can use physical or technical to beat the roll.

As much as I hate to admit it, Night City wouldn't be anything but some warlord's fiefdom without the corps around to keep things interesting. Plus, they're good customers! You sell them a shipment of faulty gear, they come back and tell you its faulty, you sell them the parts you stole from it for a markup. That's just good business, as I'm sure they'd agree!

Arasaka

Technically, Arasaka isn't allowed inside the Night City borders - treaty that ended the 4th. I very much doubt that this has stopped them from doing anything that they want to do. After all, they haven't exiled every Japanese company, and every Japanese company is part of Arasaka.

HQ/Offices

Officially, none, and if the existence of one could be proven, it would be swiftly expunged, likely at gunpoint. Which is to say, Arasaka shell companies own everything from private security firms to bodegas on the Night City streets - they just don't have ninjas in uniform on staff, anymore. They have an especially strong presence in Old Japantown, and they're always listening.

What To Expect

Arasaka agents are elite assassins and infiltrators; where Militech specializes in western firepower and impressive shows of force, Arasaka prioritizes discretion and operational intelligence. They're extremely well equipped, highly skilled, highly isolated operatives. Rather than a direct confrontation, an Arasaka cell might stalk a target for hours, days, or even months, sabotaging their efforts, setting them up for failure, creating obstacles to their plans, and only appearing at the last moment to deliver the final blow.

Corp-Wide Ability - Arasaka Counterintelligence

Arasaka agents can appear anywhere, at any time, for any reason, with motives that might completely elude all but them. Every Arasaka agent placed in a scene can be equipped with up to 1,000eb of extra gear at the GM's discretion; additionally, they're fully aware of the exact identities and true appearance of every individual in a scene, along with their personal histories and recent activities. Lastly, once per initiative round, a combination of agility, active camoflauge and deceptive movements allows an Arasaka agent to move in impossible fashion - instead of moving, they may be moved to any space on the board which is accessible to them, within spaces = double their MOVE characteristic of their previous location.

All Arasaka agents can dodge attacks using their technical skill.

Biotechnica

I'm sick of hearing these guys talked about like "the good guy corp." There are no good corps, just different ways of fucking you over. Take Biotechnica, for example - everyone sees them bringing back tigers from extinction, nobody looks at how they're being augmented as exotic pets or being sold as war beasts. They restored sections of the Sierra Nevadas, then filled them with things so dangerous that nobody can live there anymore. Now they're trying to get us to eat their mealworms and locusts - mark my words, that's Soylent Green in different packaging or my name isn't Treasure Marcus.

HQ/Offices

Biotechnica's Night City regional office is a comfortable closed campus which takes up one block of at the edge of Little Europe and the University District. They also have branch offices, observation posts, and testing facilities all over the badlands and beyond - outside of Continental Brands, they're likely one of the most widespread corps in terms of locations during the Time of the Red.

What To Expect

Biotechnica relies on generic corpos for mooks, and instead skirmishes with trained, genetically engineered animals, which can also be found feral and untameable in the foothills of the Sierra Nevada mountains. These creatures are far faster, tougher, and deadlier than anything natural evolution has ever produced, but are only marginally smarter than their natural predecessors; stay out of their nests, come prepared for them, and keep your distance.

Corp-Wide Ability - Augmented Physiology

Creatures with this ability can see in the dark. After making an attack, creatures with this ability can immediately take another action (it can't be to make a normal or aimed attack).

Militech

Ah, the golden child of the 2030s - always there with a recruitment poster to put up and a donation to a local school. Fucking neomilitarists; Militech saw Arasaka merge with the Japanese government, and got jealous that it only ended up a fourth arm of the NUSA military.

They're not technically allowed into Night City either, but they take full responsibility for staffing the NorCal military base. Officially, they patrol the badlands and put down raiders and nomad smugglers with the blessing of the government. Unofficially, they're here for the same Cold War that Arasaka's branch offices are, just much less discretely.

HQ/Offices

Militech has a large number of trained personnel on site at all times at the NorCal military basse, but the staff are only authorized to enter Night City to quell civil unrest. They have a strong presence in the Badlands, sending armored patrols out on a regular schedule to keep corporate overland trade routes clear.

What To Expect

Militech is, effectively, a military force. And unlike 6th Street, they have the funding, supplies, and manpower to live up to that title. The yanks in yellow have the capacity for everything from special ops to frontal assault, held back only by red tape and the threat of lawsuits from the inevitable collateral damage. Expect a highly tactical fight - and, if warranted, armored cavalry as backup.

Corp-Wide Ability - Advanced Tactics

Once per round after a Militech goon has taken an action, select another goon of the same type; they may immediately take the same action, if eligible to do so.

NCPD

You think the NCPD is here to protect you? Hah. The NCPD is a publicly traded corporation that's been in the red for years, and you'd better believe they're looking for unconventional sources of revenue.

Laws are just threats instituted by whoever's on top of the social hierarchy, and the hierarchy is bought and sold by corps. You connect the dots.

HQ/Offices

The NCPD main police station is located in Watson, but they patrol heavily in all the rebuilding urban zones/green districts - except for Pacifica, where corporate security and Thelas contractors keep the streets safe and quiet instead. For more details about how the NCPD responds in a particular area, see the gang-wide ability.

What To Expect

The NCPD is the long arm of the law, exercising complete discretion and utter judicial independence, and made bold by corporate backers. Their primary job in Night City is to respond to trouble calls from RatSec operatives, and to put down cyberpsychos with MaxTac. They never stop coming, and they have a long, dangerous history of holding grudges against those who challenge their authority. If you plan to work in a district where they're present, you'll want to have a bribe handy.

Gang-Wide Ability - Threat Rating

When the NCPD is first alerted to a disturbance in a district, a threat rating is immediately assigned. When the reinforcements at that tier of threat are defeated, forced to retreat, or assess the situation, the threat rating increases by 1 or more, and the reinforcements associated with that rating are immediately dispatched, arriving in 1d3 minutes.

Threat rating is not assigned in combat zones, the reclaimed perimeter or the NorCal Military Base, and cannot exceed 2 in the overpacked suburbs.

  • Tier 1: No reinforcements are sent. Created when an NCPD officer is engaged or feels threatened.

  • Tier 2 - Armed Response: two NCPD Patrol Cars are dispatched, each containing two Patrol Officers.

  • Tier 3 - Perimeter Control: three NCPD Patrol Cars are dispatched, each containing three Patrol Officers and a SWAT Officer. These officers will create a perimeter and assess the situation while attempting to prevent anyone from leaving the scene, but will not directly engage agitators unless they attempt to breach the perimeter, instead calling in a Suppression team to take down dangerous players while notifying MaxTac to remain on standby.

  • Tier 4 - Suppression Dispatch: an NCPD Armored Transport is dispatched, containing an eight-person team of SWAT Officers.

  • Tier 5 - MaxTac Response: An A/V-4 is dispatched, containing a four person team of MaxTac Officers. They do not negotiate, do not discriminate, do not take bribes, and do not take prisoners if the perps don’t surrender peacefully. They are the law.

Trauma Team

You know, back in Russia we have ambulances. They're a little slower, and the hospitals aren't as nice, but you can call one anywhere, any time, and they'll come get you - no down payment, no insurance prior to pickup, nothing. And every time I hear a bullet whiz past me, I think "the socialist pigdogs might not get much right, but at least that, they got right."

HQ/Offices

Trauma Team's NC tower is one of the last original buildings in Watson, near the NorCal military base. They also have 1-2 smaller offices around town, for admin and keeping up response times.

What To Expect

It's a well known fact that Trauma Team won't shoot at you if you don't shoot at them, and they're pretty liberal with their interpretation of "shooting at them." Because most teams are better equipped than the people they're rescuing, there's almost always a ceasefire declared when a blue and white AV roars onto the scene.

Trauma Team isn't a taxi service, though - recent policy changes only allow agents to take you if you have a membership, and only if you're actually wounded. Although this has resulted in a statistically significant increase in gunshot wounds to the legs and feet, no policy has yet emerged to quell this negative externality.

Corp-Wide Ability - Trauma Response

When a Trauma Team agent takes an action, any other agent which takes that same action towards a same or similar target/goal in that initiative round receives +2 to their check. This effect stacks.

Heavily armed and armored, Trauma Team’s arrival tends to lead to ceasefires, even within the bloodiest conflicts, as anyone who continues fighting is seen as an aggressor and dealt with accordingly. Rumors of “Trauma Diplomacy” - that just the appearance of TT leads to the parties talking it out - have likely been greatly exaggerated, though. Violence will likely resume as soon as the transport leaves. For more details about their arrival times and formations, players can see CORE p. 223-224.

It isn't just the big players that make Night City turn. There's dozens of smaller gangs and corps operating under the surface, mixing with each other and the city like potassium and water…

Bozos

Clown freaks that started innocent enough. Don't have to shoot on sight - a lot of them are still just a bunch of partying freaks with an edge, and there's a bloody civil war going on right now between the circus of joy, which is that, and the circus of merriment, which is Jigsaw and the Joker's bastard child. So maybe shoot on sight anyway.

Territory

Strong presence in the University District.

What To Expect

Bozos will transform their lairs into nightmarish locations catered towards the worst fears of their abducted targets. There's something not quite right about them… they can play with your head and leave you seeing things that aren't there, right up until they pluck out your eyes. Show no fear!

Gang-Wide Ability - Circus Freaks

Bozos have the uncanny ability to sus out people’s deepest fears courtesy of aftermarket interrogation soft, be it for the addictive thrill-seeking of merrymakers, or the twisted torture of the joyboys. When a Bozo enters a scene, every non-Bozo character must make a Willpower Save (roll 1d10 - a result less or equal to the player’s Willpower stat passes; use 1/2 lowdown for NPCs). On a failure, that character immediately loses a facedown check against every Bozo in the scene. This penalty is only removed once all Bozos are killed, rendered unconscious, or removed from a scene.

Additionally, most Bozos will enter combat under the influence of ReTox, the gang’s drug of choice. See the item description for more information.

Inquisitors

Pure-body terrorists with a religious puritan edge. Everyone hates these guys, including me! They shot up my favorite noodle shop a few weeks ago just because the owner had a prosthesis. Medical-grade! Now every time I eat there I'll have to think of that poor gonk's bloody arm-stump.

Territory

Most common on the island, but have no central base of operations; anytime one is found, the other gangs unite and burn them out.

What To Expect

Inquisitors are dangerous to borgs in particular, with the ability to disable cybernetics almost at a glance - but don't pose as much of a threat to anyone without chrome. Keep an eye on the servitors, which are slow and lumbering but can take a considerable amount of damage before going down.

Gang-Wide Ability - Cybernetic Debilitation

When rolling an attack against an enemy equipped with cybernetics, 5s count as 6s for purposes of determining critical hits. A critical hit rolled this way does not do damage, and disables a random piece of cyberware the target is equipped with instead of any other effects. Cyberware damaged in this way can be restored to functionality as an action by a Cybertech, via a DV15 Cyberware check.

Iron Sights

Used to be, the sights were another bloody boostergang like any other, funded by Arasaka. But after the Japs pulled their support, they had to rebrand, and most of them didn't survive. Now they're a group of assassins - the highly-paid mercs you hire when the edgerunners are too explosive and Arasaka's too expensive. People wonder who's funding them, but in my opinion, everyone is - every gang, every corp, all of them after the perfect contract killer.

Territory

The sights do not hold, or respect, gang territory. It's rumored that their base of operations is underneath the old city center, but that's mostly just because nobody's figured out anything about them.

What To Expect

Iron Sights members have no true identity, instead going from one skin to the next for the job. You won't find them engaging in up-front confrontation, either - they appear, silence their target, then vanish just as quickly, making them a potentially terrifyingly lethal ghost. Encounters with them will be as much detective stories as confrontations, rooting out the killers among the crowd.

Gang-Wide Ability - Concealed Identity

When placing one or more Iron Sights agents in a scene, they may disguise themselves flawlessly as any other type of mook, up to and including the acquisition of additional gear which the mook would normally be carrying. If they do so, their name changes to the other mook, they gain that mook's abilities in addition to their own, and are indistinguishable from a normal enemy with extra gear and abilities unless another character succeeds a DV21 Deduction or Conceal/Reveal Object check, indicating their true allegiances.

Philharmonic Vampyres

Hah! I like these guys; just competent enough to not come off as total posers. Like Robin Hood! Except they steal for attention and give for themselves. Still, they don't tend to kill and do have a vigilante streak. I wonder if joining the gang gives the members a better shot at going legit on stage?

Territory

Strong presence in the University District, with "shows" all over the city.

What To Expect

Modern Vampyres are known for their bombastic practical effects and ornate sets and choreography - their jobs sometimes involve months of preparation, scouting, and rehearsal. No two are alike.

Gang-Wide Ability - Audience Participation

Whether robbing a bank, brawling in the streets or supplying drugs to the city, each and every Vampyre is united by a common want of notoriety and publicity, and painstakingly organize their antics to achieve it. When setting up a scene with one or more Vampyres, roll or choose an entry from the Vampyre Set Dressing rollable table. Every Vampyre has a pool of luck (measured as COOL - thanks, Foundry) which they can use to activate effects as described in the associated journal entry. Each Vampyre starts at 1 luck.

Piranhas

Stoners, party animals and morons. I have two rules - never work with a Piranha, and never do business in a Piranha neighborhood. You'll have teams come back covered in spray paint, missing fingers, high as a kite and forgetting what they went there to do in the first place.

Territory

Strong presence in the overpacked suburbs, with footholds in Pacifica and the University District.

What To Expect

When they're mellow, Piranhas are no real threat - it's only after the party starts and hype starts to build that they become dangerous(ly stupid). After that point, they'll start succeeding at things that no sane person should ever attempt.

Gang-Wide Ability - Hype AF

In a scene with a Piranha presence, the hype meter starts at 0, and actions in the scene generate/remove hype. For each point of current hype, Piranhas gain +1 to their checks. When The hype meter reaches 5 (max), Piranhas auto-pass any checks with a DV of 15 or below, gain +5 to all others, but do not gain any further bonuses for a hype of 6+. For each point that hype drops below 0, Piranhas add a -1 modifier to their checks; when it falls to -4, the Piranhas will lose interest in the scene and wander off, come to their senses, or surrender. The hype meter has no upward cap, but stops at -4.

Prime-Time Players

Ever find yourself thinking that the world is just too much? The PTPs decided to take escapism to its next level, turning their favorite media into their lifestyle. Doesn't sound too bad, but they're one of the most confrontational gangs in the city - they hate everything and everyone who's not in on the schtick, and are sociopathically dedicated to their fucked-up worldview.

Territory

Concentrated into a few neighborhoods of the overpacked suburbs, and always looking to expand.

What To Expect

The players are known for their aggression against outsiders and their utterly surreal methods for fighting. At first glance they're nothing special, but their territory is often made up to look like whatever media the cell is imitating, up to and including traps and back corners that are fraught with hazards for anyone not in the know.

Gang-Wide Ability - Audience In A Can

Any time a Prime-Time Player succeeds an opposed check or an attack roll, canned laughter or applause will play in the background. The opposed party must make a Cool save (roll 1d10 - a result less or equal to the player's Cool stat passes; use 1/2 lowdown for NPCs); on failure, they take a -2 to their next check. This effect stacks once.

Reckoners

Another day, another terrorist cell. Why anyone would join an organization where you strap a bomb to your chest, I have no idea - but the Reckoners have a bit more going on. They're strange - I've heard from edgerunners and even in NCPD reports that the unexplainable tends to happen around them, and I'm not talking about VDB tech-mysticism or the carefully-planned performances of the Vampyres, either.

Territory

Strong presence in New Westbrook, moderate presence in Watson; appear almost everywhere in the city, though - anywhere that there's homelessness and helplessness is a ripe recruiting ground for Reckoners.

What To Expect

Reckoners are suicidally devoted to their nihilistic ideals, and will gladly throw themselves onto your sword. More strangely, though, are their occult abilities - some god of destruction smiles kindly on their devotion, it would seem. It seems counterintuitive to expect the unexpected - but here we are.

Gang-Wide Ability - Martyr

At the start of each initiative round, you may choose one Reckoner to martyr. The Reckoner immediately detonates a vest of cheap plastic explosives, killing themselves instantly. Treat this explosion as the detonation of an armor-piercing rocket, centered on the Reckoner.

Red Chrome Legion

Uuuughghhhh… I wish I could say that these guys aren't worth taking seriously, and compared to the other gangs, they're not. Red Chrome is who you join when 6th Street laughs in your face and the Tygers threaten to skin you. But even idiots with guns are still people with guns, and there's a lot of them for some reason. Do Night City a favor and bring the flamethrower the next time you raid Red Chrome - the world will be a better place for them being burned to a crisp.

Territory

The RCL is strongest in the overpacked suburbs, though most of its leaders/scammers live in Pacifica or Little Europe thanks to their followers' investments. Every other gang hates these guys, and they hate everyone else - they're at constant war with their surroundings, as well as the concept of objective reality itself.

What To Expect

RCL members aren't threatening individually, but there's a lot of them. As more and more people end up disenfranchised with society, more and more fascists appear, and there's no shortage of people waiting to take advantage of their anger, either. Expect hordes of angry white dudes, and bullets flying in every direction.

Gang-Wide Ability - Single-Minded

Each subsequent RCL member to take the same action in a round (I.E. shooting at the same target, lifting the same thing) receives +1 to their check when doing so.

Scott Brown Real Estate

Ah, the Red Decades, where there's a lot of empty buildings and a lot of homeless people on the streets. Scott Brown is the largest realtor keeping in that way, and there's enough legislation giving them authority over the squatters they find to keep it that way. Each one is a corpo to the core, fighting to keep taking rent and scamming other corps instead of just letting people live where they want. I like them a lot.

Territory

Scott Brown's NC Branch is located in Upper Marina, but they have small offices all over town. Their agents are on location all over the city, providing "forcible evictions" to gangs and the homeless when they try to sell their properties off.

What To Expect

Scott Brown agents operate either on their own or in pairs, and act as lethal agents of the long arm of Corporate America. Their abilities and kit are well suited to causing chaos, and they aren't afraid to call in the NCPD for backup if they can't handle the situation themselves.

Gang-Wide Ability - Scott Brown Motherfucker, Get The Fuck Out, I've Got A Showing!

When a Scott Brown agent enters the scene, they can choose to exclaim this line. If they were not previously known, they are placed at the top of the initiative order regardless of roll, and immediately open fire. Any actors in the scene with a rep of 2 or less must succeed a DV15 Cool Endurance check (use lowdown for NPCs). On failure, any NPCs attempt to flee, while player characters are immediately suppressed with the agent(s) in the scene acting as the source of the effect. They remain suppressed until passing the check, which they can do at the start of each of their turns.

Steel Vaqueros

I don't have much to say about the big nomad clan outside NC. Good for smuggling, always seem to be on the wrong side of Militech. Don't mess with their cars, they won't forgive you, and they're none-too-friendly to outsiders, but they're reliable and likely won't shoot at you unless you shoot first.

Territory

The Vaqueros contest control of the Badlands with armored Militech forces and scav raiders, but usually stay outside NC's borders.

What To Expect

Vaqueros rely on vehicles for everything, and if you don't have a tuned rig for yourself, you shouldn't expect to stand much of a chance against them.

Gang-Wide Ability - Moto Rollers

When a Steel Vaquero is in a scene, they're accompanied by a Generic Compact, Generic Truck, Generic Van, Buggy, or Hauler; each subsequent Vaquero adds an upgrade to the vehicle depending on their purpose in the scene. Once a vehicle has more than half of its seats occupied, you may place another.

Voodoo Boys

You don't know how hard it was to find information on the new-age VDBs. Used to be a run-of-the-mill drug gang, but a lot of actual Haitians have been moving to NC lately, since their island's slowly going underwater. They're a mystery - use their own fixers, build their own tech, I hear they've even got their own subnet. It's been less than 5 years and they're already running the drug game for half the city - and the tech I've seen from them has been incomprehensible!

Territory

The VDBs have a strong presence in Pacifica, but deal drugs and aftermarket technology across half the city.

What To Expect

The VDBs are highly internalized, centralized, and isolated, and don't take new recruits very often if they aren't blood-related. Individual cells are small, compartmentalized and crafty - each one is unique, relying on their tech and abilities in unique, original ways. Their high technical score lets them specialize in how they take apart enemies, manipulating technology within scenes to their lethal benefit. Bring EMP grenades, but not for the gang members - most only have surface-level chrome. Bring it for the possessed machinery that's sure to be present in any scene.

Gang-Wide Ability - Indistinguishable From Magic

The VDBs utilize indecipherable custom technology layered with occult mysticism, and have achieved an innate understanding of its workings. When making a check about or against an actor or object with one piece of borgware or foundational cyberware installed, VDBs gain +2 to the result. For each subsequent installation, this bonus increases by 1.

Additionally, every member of the VDBs is equipped with the ability to manipulate technology to their benefit, causing electrical surges, hacking robotics, etc. As an action, a VDB can make a technical check against an object they can see - if controlled by someone else, the check is contested (player characters use a relevant tech skill). On success, the VDB gains control of that object and may manipulate it as an incidental during each of their turns.
